message:
fix(fr-runtime): ACL LOG/CAT wrong-arity emits subcommand-syntax-error wording [bd-frankenredis-egom]

Upstream commands.def declares ACL with arity = -2, so any extra
args after a valid subcommand land in aclCommand's fallthrough
which calls addReplySubcommandSyntaxError producing
'unknown subcommand or wrong number of arguments for <SUB>. Try
ACL HELP.' The subcommand token preserves the user-typed casing
(LOG vs log).

handle_acl_log and handle_acl_cat were emitting the standard
WrongSubcommandArity wording 'wrong number of arguments for
acl|log command' instead. Replace with a new helper
acl_subcommand_syntax_error that mirrors networking.c::addReplySubcommandSyntaxError
(uses argv[1] verbatim, capped at 128 bytes, ACL parent uppercased).

Other ACL subcommands (GETUSER, DELUSER, SETUSER, etc.) declare
explicit arity in commands.def so they keep the 'acl|X command'
wording from the dispatch-time arity check — unchanged.
